comment: قُل لِّأَزْوَاجِكَ — the printed Cairo 1924 mushaf has the shadda on the لام (idgham after the sukūn of قُل); the XML source is missing it.
قُل لِّأَزْوَاجِكَ — same as 33:28, shadda missing from XML source.
ٱللَّٰعِنُونَ — Cairo XML placed the shadda on the first لام; the printed mushaf (and Madinah) place it on the second لام with the fatha+sup-alef on that same second لام.
فَأُوَٰرِيَ — Cairo XML is missing the trailing fatha on the yāʾ (subjunctive ending -iya); the printed Cairo mushaf has it.
لِّأَزْوَاجِهِم — preposition lām missing its shadda in the XML source (same idgham pattern as 33:28, 33:59); the printed Cairo mushaf has it.
خَيۡرࣱ لِّأَنفُسِهِمۡ — preposition lām missing its shadda (idgham after open dammatan); Cairo mushaf has it.
عِيدࣰا لِّأَوَّلِنَا — preposition lām missing its shadda (idgham after open fathatan); Cairo mushaf has it.
خَيۡرࣰا لِّأَنفُسِكُمۡ — preposition lām missing its shadda (idgham after open fathatan); Cairo mushaf has it.
